query: Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo virus outbreak confirmed case count latest update late June and July 2026. Have any new cases been reported after 21 June 2026 bringing Uganda total above 20? Check ECDC CDTR Week 27, WHO Disease Outbreak News.
answer:
Research complete. Here is the summary of findings on the Uganda Ebola Bundibugyo virus outbreak case count:

## Answer: Uganda remains at 20 confirmed cases — NO new cases after 21 June 2026

**Uganda's total has NOT risen above 20.** All authoritative sources consistently report **20 confirmed cases, including 2 deaths**, through at least 3 July 2026.

### Cross-validated evidence:

| Source | Date | Uganda Count | Key Detail |
|--------|------|--------------|------------|
| **ECDC** (CDTR Week 27 / outbreak page) | Updated 3 July 2026 | 20 cases, 2 deaths | "The last confirmed case was reported on 21 June and no new cases have been reported since then" |
| **WHO DON612** (after DON608 of 19 June) | Early July 2026 | 20 cases, 2 deaths | Part of 1,481 total (1,460 DRC, 20 Uganda, 1 France) |
| **ebolamap.org** (ECDC-sourced) | 26 June 2026 | 20 cases, 2 deaths | — |
| **ebola.fyi tracker** | Early July 2026 | 20 cases, 2 deaths | Border closed |
| **Wikipedia** | First week of July | 2 Uganda deaths | — |

### Key points:
- The **21 June 2026 case was the most recent Ugandan case** — an imported case that reset Uganda's countdown toward an Ebola-free declaration (per Daily Monitor).
- Of Uganda's 20 cases: **15 DRC-linked, 5 local transmission, 15 recovered**.
- Uganda's count is **static**, in sharp contrast to DRC (~1,460 cases and rising rapidly).
- The **CDTR Week 27** (27 June–3 July) window is precisely the period asked about — it confirms no new Uganda cases during that week.

**Direct answer to your question:** No, no new Uganda cases have been reported after 21 June 2026. Uganda's total remains at 20 and has not gone above 20.

sources.title: Ebola disease outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo and Uganda
sources.snippet: As of 1 July 2026, Uganda had reported a total of 20 confirmed cases, including two deaths. The last confirmed case was reported on 21 June and no new cases have been reported since then. Page last updated 3 July at 16:45.

sources.title: Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo & Uganda (DON612)
sources.snippet: In total, 1481 confirmed cases; 1460 from the Democratic Republic of the Congo, 20 from Uganda and one from France (linked to DRC); and 454 deaths including two from Uganda, have been reported.

sources.title: 2026 Ebola epidemic
sources.snippet: There have been 452 confirmed deaths in D.R. Congo, and two deaths in Uganda as of the first week of July. Declared a PHEIC by WHO on 16 May 2026; caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus.

sources.title: Ebola Map — Live Global Ebola Outbreak Tracker
sources.snippet: As of 26 June: DRC 1,155 confirmed cases and 304 confirmed deaths; Uganda 20 confirmed cases and 2 deaths; outbreak total 1,175 cases and 306 deaths (CFR 26%). Source: ECDC.

sources.title: Ebola Outbreak Tracker — 2026 Bundibugyo Strain
sources.snippet: Uganda 20c / 2d · 1 child positive · BORDER CLOSED. DRC 1,307c / 377d.

sources.title: Ebola countdown in Uganda collapses after 16 days
sources.snippet: Uganda's hopes of securing an Ebola-free declaration have suffered a setback after health authorities confirmed a new imported Ebola case on June 21, effectively resetting the country's countdown towards ending the outbreak.

sources.title: Situational update on the Ebola disease outbreak caused by Bundibugyo virus (26 June 2026)
sources.snippet: Outbreak declared 15 May 2026 in DRC and Uganda, ongoing. On 24 June 2026, France reported its first confirmed case in a returning healthcare worker.

sources.title: Current information about Ebola | RIVM
sources.snippet: Update 24 June 2026: Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) confirmed in a healthcare worker in France who worked in the DRC; placed in isolation.

key_facts: Uganda's Ebola (Bundibugyo virus) total is 20 confirmed cases including 2 deaths as of 1-3 July 2026.
No new Uganda cases have been reported after 21 June 2026; the total has NOT risen above 20.
ECDC (page updated 3 July 2026, covering CDTR Week 27, 27 June-3 July): 'The last confirmed case was reported on 21 June and no new cases have been reported since then.'
WHO Disease Outbreak News DON612 reports 20 Uganda cases and 2 Uganda deaths within an overall total of 1,481 confirmed cases and 454 deaths across DRC, Uganda, and France.
Of Uganda's 20 cases: 15 linked to DRC, 5 local transmission, 15 recovered (per ECDC, as of 1 July 2026).
The 21 June 2026 Uganda case was an imported case that reset Uganda's countdown toward an Ebola-free declaration (Daily Monitor).
The outbreak is caused by Bundibugyo ebolavirus and was declared a PHEIC by WHO on 16 May 2026.
DRC accounts for the overwhelming majority of cases (~1,460 as of 2 July 2026) and continues to rise rapidly, unlike Uganda's static count.
France reported one confirmed case (a returning healthcare worker) on 24 June 2026, linked to DRC.

data_points.metric: Overall outbreak confirmed cases (DRC + Uganda + France)
